vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The visual data indicates a critical failure of the expected biome. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), P3 (Pothos), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visible as living specimens. The pots appear to contain only substrate and debris.

plant_audit.P1: No foliage detected. Pot contains only dark, possibly desiccated soil.

vision_report.biome_observations: The desk surface shows significant debris. There is a spoon present in the earliest images, which is later removed. The soil in all pots appears uniformly dark and lacks any signs of active growth, moss, or secondary seedlings. The environment is currently non-viable for the registered species.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to CURRENT, the scene shows a progressive loss of objects (spoon removal) and a complete absence of biological activity. No plant growth or recovery has occurred; the state remains static and barren throughout the 5-day sequence.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: All specimens are confirmed as 'Dead/Lost'. The lack of any green biomass or structural integrity in the pots suggests a total collapse of the botanical system.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of a spoon in the early images is an uncatalogued intervention. The primary anomaly is the total absence of the registered plants, suggesting either total removal or complete decomposition/desiccation prior to the start of the observation window.
vision_report.narrative_description: Upon meticulous audit of the provided image stack, I have determined that the biome is currently in a state of total systemic failure. The expected plants (P1-P4) are entirely absent from the pots. The white rabbit anchor remains in P3, but the Pothos is missing. The soil appears dark and lifeless. The removal of the spoon between T-3 and T-2 indicates human intervention, but no botanical recovery has been observed. The environment is currently devoid of any living plant matter.
